macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Decline Amid Iran Conflict, S&P 500 Nears Longest Losing Streak
items.summary: US stocks fell on March 20, 2026, with the S&P 500 down 0.5% in early trading toward its longest losing streak in a year, Dow dropping 0.3%, and Nasdaq slipping 0.8%, driven by rising Treasury yields and inflation fears from the Iran war. Oil prices eased slightly with Brent at $108.29 and WTI at $95.53, while Super Micro Computer shares plunged 28.2% over smuggling allegations.[1]

items.title: Russell 2000 Enters Correction Territory as Iran War Drags On
items.summary: The Russell 2000 fell into correction territory on March 20, 2026, its first since January 2025, amid the ongoing Iran conflict now in its fourth week; Nasdaq dropped 2% but avoided correction, while S&P 500 closed at 6,506.48 down 1.9% for the week. Energy sector gained 3%, but technical indicators show weakening support for stocks.[2]

items.title: S&P 500 Down 0.84% to 6,551.67 as Markets Gyrate on Iran Tensions
items.summary: On March 20, 2026, the S&P 500 declined 0.84% to 6,551.67 with more decliners than advancers, as the Iran conflict continued to unsettle markets; weekly losses included S&P 500 -1.9%, Nasdaq -2.1%, and Dow -2.1% to 45,577.47. Traders shifted from rate cut bets to possible 2026 hike amid inflation concerns.[3][1]

items.title: Rising Treasury Yields and Inflation Fears Reshape Fed Rate Expectations
items.summary: Treasury yields climbed due to Iran conflict risks to energy supplies via Strait of Hormuz, leading traders to abandon multiple 2026 rate cuts and price in a small rate hike probability per CME data. This pressures stock valuations and borrowing costs amid policy uncertainty.[1]

items.title: Super Micro Computer Shares Plunge 28% on Smuggling Charges
items.summary: Super Micro Computer dropped 28.2% after US authorities accused a senior executive and affiliates of conspiring to smuggle advanced Nvidia servers to China, amid broader market declines.[1]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Pi Network Price Surges Over 25% on Protocol Upgrade Progress
items.summary: Pi Network (PI) gained over 25% in 24 hours following the successful migration to Protocol 20 and anticipation for v21 upgrade with improved KYC and mainnet migration. Daily trading volume reached 18 million PI amid broader market up 0.51%.[2][3]

items.title: FET Rockets 17% on AI Sector Momentum and OpenAI News
items.summary: Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (FET) rose over 17% in 24 hours, fueled by AI hype from OpenAI's new model releases, with $176M+ daily volume and 38% two-week gains. AI and RWA sectors outperformed amid altcoin divergence.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin Tests $70K Support Amid Extreme Fear and Bearish Signals
items.summary: BTC trades around $70,619 (+0.35%) testing $70K support with extreme fear in markets, +12,450 BTC exchange inflows (bearish), paused whale accumulation, and SOPR at 0.97 indicating loss realization. Altcoin index down 38% from 2026 highs.[1][2]

items.title: TRON and Solana Show Resilience as Top Performers
items.summary: TRON (TRX) up 1.26% to $0.309 with USDT supply at 58% ($67B) and $4.2B stablecoin transfers; Solana (SOL) +0.16% to $89.96 with $1.8B DEX volume and 99.97% uptime. Both outperform amid market downturn.[1]

items.title: Playnance G Coin Launch Sees Rapid Staking Growth
items.summary: Playnance's G Coin transitioned from presale to trading with over 1 billion tokens staked shortly after launch, holder count tripling to 623k, and 3.2B tokens locked signaling strong early demand in gaming and prediction markets.[4]

items.title: SEC/CFTC Joint Guidance and ETF Filings Boost Optimism
items.summary: New SEC/CFTC regulatory guidance and ETF filings, including potential spot XRP ETF vote on March 27, add optimism as BTC holds above $70,800 and market cap reaches $2.42T. Upcoming Bitcoin $75K options expiry may increase volatility.[3]

items.title: Hong Kong's Web3 Framework Draws Crypto Firms Back
items.summary: Hong Kong's maturing regulatory clarity for digital assets and Web3, including stablecoin rules, is attracting global crypto firms and figures to the city.[6]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ubisoft Shuts Down Game Development at Red Storm Entertainment, Laying Off 105 Employees
items.summary: Ubisoft has ended game development at its Tom Clancy studio Red Storm Entertainment, resulting in 105 layoffs as part of ongoing cost-cutting measures and restructuring. This follows earlier layoffs, game cancellations, and studio closures earlier in 2026.
items.source: Insider Gaming via Smash JT YouTube

items.title: Crystal Dynamics Hit with Another Round of Layoffs
items.summary: Crystal Dynamics has laid off around 20 employees, contributing to the ongoing 'GAME CRASH 2026' with multiple studio impacts. This is part of broader industry challenges reported on March 19-20, 2026.

items.title: NVIDIA Introduces DLSS 5 with AI-Driven Visual Enhancements
items.summary: NVIDIA has launched DLSS 5, shifting toward AI-driven visual quality improvements in games. This was highlighted in gaming news updates for March 20, 2026.

items.title: NCSoft Shifts to Player-Driven Development, Reduces Pay-to-Win Elements
items.summary: NCSoft announced a move to player-driven development decisions and less reliance on pay-to-win systems. This industry development was covered in March 20 gaming news.

items.title: Steam Spring Sale 2026 Launches
items.summary: The Steam Spring Sale 2026 has begun, alongside multiple game releases scheduled from March 21 to 25. Updates for Helldivers 2, Zenless Zone Zero, and Pokémon TCG Pocket's Mega Shine expansion were also noted.

items.title: Mobile Legends Revives Naruto Collaboration Launching March 27
items.summary: Mobile Legends is bringing back its Naruto series collaboration in March 2026, featuring Gamakichi's advanced sacred statue and themed effects, set to launch on March 27.

items.title: Pirates Crack Latest Denubu-Protected Games
items.summary: Pirates have begun cracking recent Denubu-protected games, signaling ongoing adaptations in anti-tamper protection for 2025-2026 releases.

items.title: Beyblade X Announces World Championships and Roblox Partnership
items.summary: Beyblade X revealed international tournaments starting April 2026 with a GP Final in Bangkok, plus a Roblox digital championship from May 15-25 commemorating 100 million visits. New products were also announced on March 21.

items.title: Anbernic Prepares RG Vita and RG Vita Pro Handheld Pre-Orders
items.summary: Anbernic is set to launch its RG Vita and RG Vita Pro emulation handhelds, with pre-orders opening on March 23, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SoftBank Plans Massive Gas-Fired Power Plant in Ohio for AI Energy Needs
items.summary: Japan's SoftBank Group announced plans to build a huge new gas-fired power plant in the US state of Ohio to provide energy for artificial intelligence operations.

items.title: Micron Technology Reports Extraordinary Q2 Earnings Beat
items.summary: Micron Technology delivered exceptional Q2 results with EPS of $12.20 versus $8.66 consensus and revenue of $23.86B versus $19.74B expected, representing 196.4% revenue growth.

items.title: Drone Manufacturer's Office Set on Fire in Czech Republic
items.summary: A fire broke out on March 20 at an industrial facility belonging to LPP Holding, a company linked to defense projects, in the Czech city of Pardubice.

items.title: United Airlines Trims Flights as Jet Fuel Costs Spike
items.summary: United Airlines is reducing flight schedules in response to rising jet fuel costs.
